text: Great Smoky Mountains National Park
zh: 大烟山国家公园

text: America, as the song goes, is purple mountain majesties.
zh: 歌中唱道，美国是紫色山峦的壮丽之地。

text: And none more majestic than those of Great Smoky Mountains National Park.
zh: 而没有哪里比大烟山国家公园更为壮丽。

text: Sprawling over half a million acres across the borders of two states, Tennessee and North Carolina,
zh: 这里横跨田纳西州和北卡罗来纳州两州边界，占地超过五十万英亩，

text: Great Smoky Mountains National Park straddles, ridge upon ridge, of some of the of the oldest mountains in the world.
zh: 大烟山国家公园群峰连绵，是世界上最古老的山脉之一。

text: To the Cherokee, the Native Americans of this region, these were the shakana, meaning blue like smoke.
zh: 对这片区域的原住民切罗基人来说，这里被称为“沙卡纳”，意为“如烟般的蓝色”。

text: Rising mists veil the mountain heights. lays sleepily in the valleys
zh: 缭绕的雾气笼罩山巅，静静地沉睡在山谷中，

text: and clings to the forests like ghostly shrouds. We call them the Smokies.
zh: 又如幽灵般的裹尸布缠绕森林。我们称它们为“烟雾山”。

text: And to those who penetrate its foggy curtain, it's a place of
zh: 而对于那些穿透这层雾幕的人来说，这里是一个

text: forests are ancient, survivors of the Ice Age.
zh: 古老森林的所在，是冰河时代遗留下来的幸存者。

text: In 1830, the United States government forced the Cherokee Native Americans of North Carolina and Tennessee, in dreadful weather,
zh: 1830年，美国政府在恶劣天气下强迫北卡罗来纳州和田纳西州的切罗基原住民，

text: and with little time and much anguish, to gather their belongings and relocate to a faraway territory unknown to them.
zh: 在时间紧迫、痛苦万分之中，收拾家当，迁往一个他们从未知晓的遥远之地。

text: But a handful of Cherokees, led by a renegade warrior, disobeyed the edict and hid in the the groves of this great forest.
zh: 但一小部分由一名反叛勇士带领的切罗基人不服从这一法令，躲藏在这片大森林的丛林深处。

text: Then came a large -scale logging operation. More than 90 % of the region's trees were
zh: 随后，大规模的伐木作业开始了。这一地区超过90%的树木被

text: felled. Alarmed, the United States Congress authorized the park in 1926.
zh: 砍伐。美国国会为此深感忧虑，于1926年批准设立该公园。

text: protects one of the few old -growth forests in eastern North America.
zh: 如今，它保护着北美东部为数不多的原始森林之一。

text: Today a scenic highway, the new found Gap Road provides access to unforgettable sights.
zh: 如今，一条风景优美的公路——新发现峡谷路，通向令人难忘的景致。

text: nights. One might even observe the park's prodigal elk.
zh: 在夜晚，人们甚至可能观察到公园里那曾经消失又重现的麋鹿。

text: have reappeared in the Great Smokies.
zh: 它们又重新出现在大烟山中。

text: Once abundant, they were wiped out by hunting and by the loss of their wild grazing grounds.
zh: 曾经数量众多，却因狩猎和野生牧场的丧失而几乎绝迹。

text: The last elk in these environs was killed in the late 1700s.
zh: 这一带最后一头麋鹿在18世纪末被猎杀。

text: In the present 21st century, elk have been reintroduced to the area and they are thriving.
zh: 如今在21世纪，麋鹿已被重新引入这片区域，并繁衍兴盛。

text: Visitors come here for renewal. Or to test themselves.
zh: 游客来到这里寻求心灵的更新，或是挑战自我，

text: To see if they can still meet the challenge of the wilderness.
zh: 看看自己是否还能应对荒野的考验。

text: The park offers some 850 miles of trails and unpaved roads for hiking.
zh: 公园提供约850英里的步道和未铺装的道路供徒步。

text: The rewards are the making of fond memories,
zh: 这些回报，成就了美好的回忆——

text: flowering trees, wild flowers in bloom,
zh: 盛开的花树，绽放的野花，

text: waterfalls, glimpses of elk or deer,
zh: 飞瀑流泉，偶尔瞥见的麋鹿或鹿群，

text: of black bears, and wild turkeys.
zh: 黑熊，以及野生火鸡。

text: One trail leads to Chimney Tops, a twin summit so named for its double capstone peaks.
zh: 有一条小径通往“烟囱顶”，因其双峰状的岩顶而得名。

text: Some come for sport, exploring on horseback.
zh: 有些人是为了骑马探险而来。

text: Abounding brooks and streams attract fishermen and
zh: 溪流纵横，吸引着垂钓的男男女女。

text: fisherwomen. Brook trout are native to these waters.
zh: 溪红点鲑是这些水域的原生鱼种。

text: come to discover how their forefathers lived.
zh: 人们来这里，是为了了解祖先当年的生活方式。

text: Here history is preserved and here it can be relived.
zh: 在这里，历史被保存下来，也在这里重新被唤醒。

text: These are the log cabins and frame houses where the first white settlers lived.
zh: 这些是最早的白人定居者居住过的木屋和框架房屋，

text: Going back almost 200 years to 1819.
zh: 时间可追溯到近200年前的1819年。

text: Here they planted crops, and harvested,
zh: 他们在这里播种、收获，

text: and raised children, and bred livestock.
zh: 抚养孩子，饲养牲畜。

text: Cades Cove is a broad verdant valley surrounded by mountains and is one of the most popular destinations
zh: 凯德斯谷是一片被群山环绕的宽广翠绿山谷，是大烟山最受欢迎的

text: in the Great Smokies. Scattered along the 11 -mile one -way loop road are three churches,
zh: 景点之一。沿着这条11英里长的单行环形道路，散布着三座教堂、

text: a working grist mill, mill, barns, log houses,
zh: 一座仍在运转的磨坊、谷仓、木屋，

text: and many other faithfully restored 18th and 19th century structures.
zh: 以及许多其他被精心修复的18、19世纪建筑。

text: They worshipped in simple churches, like Palmer Chapel
zh: 他们曾在简朴的教堂中做礼拜，比如卡塔卢奇谷中的

text: in Cataloochee Valley.
zh: 帕尔默教堂。

text: is where they ground their grain. A water powered grist mill.
zh: 这里是他们磨谷物的地方——一座靠水力驱动的磨坊。

text: This mill is still operating.
zh: 这座磨坊至今仍在运转。

text: On warm summer nights in the Elkmont area of the park, there is what has been called by those who've seen it,
zh: 在公园埃尔克蒙特地区温暖的夏夜里，见过的人都称之为

text: a light show. A firefly light show.
zh: 一场灯光秀——萤火虫的灯光秀。

text: Hundreds of the park's 14 species of firefly sequin the night with their tinkling luminescence.
zh: 公园中14种萤火虫里，成百上千只闪烁着微光，为夜空缀满点点繁星。

text: The Smokies cast their spell. Charmed visitors end their stays
zh: 大烟山施展着它的魔力，让为之倾倒的游客在离开时

text: knowing Great Smoky Mountains National Park as a realm of of rare enchantment.
zh: 深深体会到，大烟山国家公园是一片充满奇妙魅力的仙境。
